core_principles: Fusion over Imitation: Mix tactical dashboard density with consumer app fluidity
Content is King: Data density with high scanability
Negative Space: Use whitespace to group, not just lines
No Left Borders: Avoid the generic SaaS 'alert card' trope
anti_patterns: Inter everywhere (Mix fonts if possible, or use weight heavily)
Gradient Overload (Keep it matte and clean)

NeedsAttentionCard.description: Replace the multiple detached cards with a single, cohesive 'Intel Panel'. This panel groups alerts internally using subtle headers and dividers rather than separating them into different boxes. This mimics a 'Flight Plan' or 'Server Log' aesthetic—high density, high signal.

structure.styles: bg-white rounded-xl border border-slate-200 shadow-sm overflow-hidden
px-6 py-4 border-b border-slate-100 flex items-center justify-between bg-slate-50/30
structure.content: Title 'NEEDS ATTENTION' with a pulsing amber dot and a total count badge.

structure.styles: px-6 py-2 bg-slate-50/80 flex items-center gap-2
structure.content: Category Icon + Category Name (Uppercase, tracking-widest, text-[10px] text-slate-500)

structure.styles: group flex items-center justify-between px-6 py-3 hover:bg-slate-50 transition-colors cursor-default
structure.content: Athlete Name (Bold) + Description (Muted) | Actions (Visible on Hover or formatted as ghost buttons)

visual_details.badges: Do not use pill badges for the category anymore (since we have group headers). Use 'Priority Dots' or 'Status Rings' next to the athlete name if needed.
visual_details.actions: Use 'Ghost' style buttons. Text-based 'Nudge' or 'View' that appear on the right side. On mobile, show icon only.

interaction_rules.hover: Row background darkens slightly (slate-50). Action buttons increase opacity.
interaction_rules.click: Entire row is clickable (navigate to athlete). Prevent propagation on action buttons.

instructions_to_main_agent: 2. Remove the `groupAlerts` mapping that creates multiple separate <div> cards.
3. Create a single outer <section> or <div> container.
4. Inside, map through the groups but render them as 'Section Headers' within one continuous list.
5. Use `border-l-0` (remove left borders). Rely on the Group Header to distinguish categories.
6. Ensure the 'Empty State' (All Clear) matches the new clean aesthetic (maybe a centered minimalist icon).

general: Do not use generic centered layouts.
Create depth through z-index and subtle borders, not heavy shadows.
Don't align text to center globally.
Use 'sonner' for toasts if needed (though not required here).
